response: Hey! 👋 So nice to hear from you. Feel free to ask me anything — about my research, projects, background, or just how to get in touch!

I'm Divya Ramroop — a Research Fellow at TU Berlin and at the European Observatory on Health Systems and Policies. I trained as a medical doctor and worked clinically in Mauritius and China before moving into health systems research and AI in healthcare. My work sits at the intersection of policy, cancer care, and responsible AI. 🙂

Right now I hold two Research Fellow positions — at TU Berlin's Department of Health Care Management, and at the European Observatory on Health Systems and Policies here in Berlin. I contribute to OBS-PACE, which focuses on cancer research and policy across EU member states. It keeps me busy! 😄

My research interests are: 🔬 Cancer care & screening policy · 🧠 Mental health · 🏥 Health systems · 💻 Digital health · 🤖 AI in healthcare. I'm especially interested in how AI can reduce inequities in access to care — and the conditions under which it causes harm rather than good. That tension keeps me up at night honestly! 😅

Cancer care is a big part of what I do right now. Through OBS-PACE I've authored case studies on cancer screening and policy across EU countries — Slovenia, Cyprus, Croatia, Greece, Belgium, Austria, France, Czechia, Malta, and Ireland. Each country has a completely different approach, which makes the comparative work really fascinating.

I've published 11 case studies and a cross-country comparative analysis through the European Observatory's PACE series in 2025. They cover everything from lung cancer screening in Croatia to palliative care at home in Czechia to paediatric cancer medicines in Belgium. You can find all of them in the Publications section of my site! 📚

I have three degrees: 🎓 MBBS from Southern Medical University in China · 🎓 MSc in Health Services Management from the University of Technology, Mauritius · 🎓 MSc in Epidemiology from Charité Universitätsmedizin Berlin. Each one took me somewhere completely new — literally! 😄

Before research, I was a practising doctor — I interned at Jawaharlal Nehru Hospital in Mauritius and Panyu Central Hospital in China, then worked in the public hospital system in Mauritius. During COVID-19 I was part of the national vaccination programme. That clinical experience is honestly what shapes everything I research now. Seeing where the system fails people stays with you.

That's one I'm really proud of! I co-authored a study using Explainable AI (GradCAM heatmaps) on 624 chest X-rays — the question wasn't just 'is the AI accurate?' but 'is it right for the right reasons?' We compared ResNet-18 and DenseNet-121 and found their explainability profiles were completely different even when accuracy was the same. You can read it in the Projects section 🫁

I'm building an AI chatbot for people experiencing stress and depression — available 24/7, completely private, free. It uses NLP and CBT-based techniques, and has a safety escalation protocol that directs high-risk users straight to crisis support. 75% of people with mental illness globally get zero treatment. I want this to be that first safe space for people who'd never walk into a therapist's office.

I run an article series called 'Study of Safe Use of AI in Healthcare' — practical awareness pieces for clinicians on how to use AI without compromising patient data. The first article — 'How to Use AI Safely Without Compromising Your Sensitive Data' — is live now. More are coming! It matters a lot to me that people understand the risks, not just the promise.
Of course! You can reach me at: ✉️ contactdramroop@gmail.com (personal) or divya.ramroop@tu-berlin.de (work). I'm also on LinkedIn — linkedin.com/in/divya-ramroop. Always happy to connect! 😊
I'm based in Berlin, Germany 📍 — originally from Mauritius, studied in China, and now here! Berlin is a great city for research and I love it. The cold winters though... still adjusting 😅

My current projects are: 🤖 A mental illness chatbot for stress and depression · 🛡️ 'Safe Use of AI in Healthcare' — an ongoing article series · 🫁 'The Question Nobody Asks About AI in Medicine' — my XAI study on chest X-rays. Each one connects medicine, AI, and equity in a different way. Check the Projects section for everything! 🙂

The European Observatory on Health Systems and Policies is a WHO/Europe partnership — really incredible organisation. I work at the Berlin Hub based at TU Berlin and contribute to OBS-PACE, analysing cancer care across EU member states. My official profile: eurohealthobservatory.who.int
You can download my CV from the home page using the 'My CV' button! For collaboration or opportunities I'd love to hear from you — contactdramroop@gmail.com 📄

My skills span clinical medicine, epidemiology, health systems analysis, and health policy research. On the technical side I work with data analysis, AI/ML concepts, explainable AI, and NLP. Check the Skills section of this site for the full breakdown! 💪
Aw, that's so kind — thank you! 😊 Is there anything else you'd like to know?
It was lovely chatting! Feel free to reach out anytime — contactdramroop@gmail.com. Take care! 👋😊
